pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile and run against Java 17 semantics, failing to utilize Java 21 features or enforce Java 21 compatibility.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot version from 2.6.13 to 3.3.13 to support Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21; attempting to run or compile with Java 21 will result in runtime errors or incompatibilities with the older framework version.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Lombok version to a release compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older Lombok versions may fail to process annotations correctly on Java 21 due to internal API changes in the JDK compiler.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin to a version that supports the 'release' flag and Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older compiler plugins may not fully support Java 21 compilation or the 'release' configuration option.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace explicit source/target configuration with the 'release' flag for cleaner cross-compilation support.
pom.xml.why_exists: Using 'source' and 'target' separately is deprecated in favor of 'release' for ensuring API compatibility with the target JDK.
